nodes.title: RoadRaven v0.5
nodes.notes:
# RoadRaven v1.1 Development Roadmap

Electrobun desktop app for creating, editing, and live-monitoring visual roadmap trees.

**Core Value:** Nodes in the tree reflect real-time state of external systems through a pluggable integration layer — turning any JSON roadmap into a live progress dashboard.

**Stack:** Electrobun, Bun, React, TypeScript, Vitest, Playwright, react-d3-tree, CodeMirror 6, Zod, Zustand

**Progress:** 7 of 22 plans completed across 6 phases.

**✅ Status:** v1.0 milestone shipped — all six phases (scaffold through agentic authoring) are complete; post-release polish continues.

children.title: Phase 00 — App Scaffold (Prerequisite)
children.notes:
# Prerequisite: App Scaffold

Electrobun shell boots to a blank window with monorepo structure, typed RPC skeleton, and TDD pipeline fully operational on CI.

**Branch:** `gsd/phase-00-app-scaffold`
**Plans:** 3/3 complete

## Requirements
- SCAF-01: Monorepo with `packages/core`, `packages/react`, `packages/desktop`, `plugins/`
- SCAF-02: Workspace scripts resolve from root
- SCAF-03: Typed RPC boundary between Bun and webview
- SCAF-04: Stub packages exist
- SCAF-05: Biome linting configured
- SCAF-06: Vitest + Playwright configured
- SCAF-07: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
- SCAF-08: Plugin placeholder directory
- SCAF-09: Updater channel fallback

**✅ Status:** Electrobun monorepo shell boots to a blank window with a typed RPC skeleton and a green Biome→Vitest→Playwright→CI pipeline.

children.title: Plan 00-01: Monorepo Bootstrap
children.notes:
# Monorepo Bootstrap

Establish the workspace-based monorepo structure with all required packages.

**Requirements:** SCAF-01, SCAF-02, SCAF-04, SCAF-08

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `bun install` resolves all workspace dependencies
- `bun run dev` boots the app
- `bun run build:canary` produces a build
- All workspaces discoverable

**✅ Status:** Workspace restructured into a monorepo with code migrated to packages/desktop and bundleCEF enabled.

children.title: Create monorepo structure
children.notes:
Create `packages/core`, `packages/react`, `packages/desktop`, `plugins/` directory layout.

**✅ Status:** Create monorepo structure — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Migrate existing code to packages/desktop
children.notes:
Move all existing source code into the `packages/desktop` workspace.

**✅ Status:** Migrate existing code to packages/desktop — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Rewrite root package.json as workspace
children.notes:
Configure Bun workspaces in root `package.json` pointing to all packages.

**✅ Status:** Rewrite root package.json as workspace — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Create stub packages and plugin placeholder
children.notes:
Stub `@roadraven/core` and `@roadraven/react` packages with package.json and entry points. Create `plugins/` directory with README placeholder.

**✅ Status:** Create stub packages and plugin placeholder — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Plan 00-02: RPC Contract + Updater Fix
children.notes:
# RPC Contract + Updater Fix

Establish the typed RPC boundary and fix Updater crash.

**Requirements:** SCAF-03, SCAF-09
**Depends on:** 00-01

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- TypeScript compiles with typed RPC contract
- `Updater.localInfo.channel()` defaults to `"dev"` when `version.json` absent
- RPC types importable from both Bun and webview sides

**✅ Status:** shared/types.ts defines the typed RoadmapRPCType contract and the Updater.localInfo.channel wrapper fix landed.

children.title: Create shared/types.ts with RoadmapRPCType
children.notes:
Define the typed RPC contract in `shared/types.ts` with `RoadmapRPCType` covering all cross-process calls.

**✅ Status:** Create shared/types.ts with RoadmapRPCType — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Fix Updater.localInfo.channel() wrapper
children.notes:
Wrap `Updater.localInfo.channel()` in try/catch. Default to `"dev"` when `version.json` is absent to prevent crash on fresh installs.

**✅ Status:** Fix Updater.localInfo.channel() wrapper — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Establish typed RPC boundary
children.notes:
Ensure RPC types are importable from both Bun main process and webview renderer. Single source of truth in `shared/types.ts`.

**✅ Status:** Establish typed RPC boundary — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Plan 00-03: TDD Pipeline
children.notes:
# TDD Pipeline

Configure the complete testing and CI infrastructure.

**Requirements:** SCAF-05, SCAF-06, SCAF-07
**Depends on:** 00-01, 00-02

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `bunx vitest run` passes all tests
- `bunx biome check` passes lint
- Playwright both tiers (UI + process) pass
- GitHub Actions CI workflow passes on test PR

**✅ Status:** Biome, Vitest, two-tier Playwright, and GitHub Actions CI are all configured and passing.

children.title: Configure Biome linting (workspace-level)
children.notes:
Set up Biome at workspace root with rules covering all packages. Verify `bunx @biomejs/biome lint` runs clean.

**✅ Status:** Configure Biome linting (workspace-level) — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Configure Vitest with first passing suite
children.notes:
Set up Vitest configuration. Write first passing test suites covering SCAF-01, SCAF-03, SCAF-04, SCAF-08, SCAF-09 requirements.

**✅ Status:** Configure Vitest with first passing suite — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Configure Playwright two-tier tests
children.notes:
Set up Playwright with two tiers: UI tests (webview interactions) and process tests (Bun main process lifecycle).

**✅ Status:** Configure Playwright two-tier tests — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Create GitHub Actions CI workflow
children.notes:
CI pipeline: lint → typecheck → unit test → e2e. All steps must pass on test PR before merging.

**✅ Status:** Create GitHub Actions CI workflow — completed and verified during Phase 00.

children.title: Phase 01 — Visual Foundation & Themes
children.notes:
# Phase 01: Visual Foundation & Themes

App shell renders the intended design with all three built-in themes switchable, using only `--rv-*` CSS custom property tokens.

**Branch:** `gsd/phase-01-visual-foundation-themes`
**Depends on:** Phase 00 (App Scaffold)
**Plans:** 3/3 complete

## Requirements
- THEME-01: Three built-in themes (dark, light, high-contrast)
- THEME-02: `--rv-*` CSS custom property token system
- THEME-03: ThemeProvider + Zustand store + OS preference detection
- THEME-04: All app shell components use theme tokens
- THEME-05: Per-schema themeConfig overrides

**✅ Status:** App shell renders the target design across all three built-in themes using only --rv-* tokens, with per-schema themeConfig overrides and LogTape logging in place.

children.title: Plan 01-01: Tailwind v4 + Token System + ThemeProvider
children.notes:
# Tailwind v4 Migration + Token System + ThemeProvider

Migrate to Tailwind CSS v4, define the complete `--rv-*` token system, and implement ThemeProvider.

**Requirements:** THEME-01, THEME-02, THEME-03

**Deliverables:**
- `ThemeProvider.tsx` — React context provider
- `themeStore.ts` — Zustand store for theme state
- `useTheme` hook — consumer API
- `index.css` with `@theme` directive and all token definitions
- `rpc.ts` — Electroview initialization + settings persistence

**Done When:**
- App visually matches design reference
- All three themes switch without page reload
- System preference reflects `prefers-color-scheme`
- Per-schema `themeConfig` overrides work
- Zero hardcoded colour values in component CSS

**✅ Status:** Tailwind v4 migration, the --rv-* token system, ThemeProvider, and the Zustand settings store are all in place.

children.title: Migrate to Tailwind CSS v4
children.notes:
Upgrade from Tailwind v3 to v4. Update config, PostCSS setup, and migrate any existing utility classes.

**✅ Status:** Migrate to Tailwind CSS v4 — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Define --rv-* token system
children.notes:
Create complete `--rv-*` CSS custom property token set for dark, light, and high-contrast themes. Covers surfaces, text, borders, accents, status colors, and semantic tokens.

**✅ Status:** Define --rv-* token system — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Implement ThemeProvider + Zustand store
children.notes:
Create `ThemeProvider.tsx` with React context, `themeStore.ts` with Zustand, and `useTheme` hook. Detect OS `prefers-color-scheme` and persist selection.

**✅ Status:** Implement ThemeProvider + Zustand store — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Settings persistence via Electrobun RPC
children.notes:
Create `rpc.ts` with Electroview initialization. Persist theme selection across sessions via Electrobun RPC calls.

**✅ Status:** Settings persistence via Electrobun RPC — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Per-schema themeConfig overrides
children.notes:
When a roadmap schema contains a `themeConfig` block, override the active theme's tokens for that file only.

**✅ Status:** Per-schema themeConfig overrides — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Plan 01-02: App Shell Components
children.notes:
# App Shell Components

Build all core UI components with theme token integration.

**Requirements:** THEME-04
**Depends on:** 01-01

**Deliverables:**
- TopBar, Sidebar, Canvas, Node, SidePanel, StatusBar, ConfigPanel components
- All wired to `--rv-*` theme tokens
- Component tests passing

**Done When:**
- Visual design matches reference
- All components render with correct token application
- Component tests pass

**✅ Status:** All app-shell components (TopBar, Sidebar, Canvas, Node, SidePanel, StatusBar, ConfigPanel) are built, theme-token-wired, and tested.

children.title: Build TopBar component
children.notes:
App header with title, menu triggers, and theme switcher. Uses `--rv-*` tokens exclusively.

**✅ Status:** Build TopBar component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Build Sidebar component
children.notes:
Collapsible sidebar for tree navigation, file list, and settings access.

**✅ Status:** Build Sidebar component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Build Canvas component
children.notes:
Main viewport for the react-d3-tree renderer. Handles pan/zoom and node selection.

**✅ Status:** Build Canvas component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Build RoadmapNode component
children.notes:
Custom foreignObject node renderer for react-d3-tree. Shows title, status badge, type indicator.

**✅ Status:** Build RoadmapNode component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Build SidePanel component
children.notes:
Resizable detail panel showing selected node info, markdown notes, and metadata.

**✅ Status:** Build SidePanel component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Build StatusBar component
children.notes:
Bottom bar showing file path, node count, save status, and current theme.

**✅ Status:** Build StatusBar component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Build ConfigPanel component
children.notes:
Settings panel for theme selection, layout preferences, and app configuration.

**✅ Status:** Build ConfigPanel component — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Wire theme tokens to all components
children.notes:
Ensure every component references `--rv-*` tokens. Zero hardcoded colour values.

**✅ Status:** Wire theme tokens to all components — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Write component tests
children.notes:
Unit tests for each component verifying render, theme token application, and interactions.

**✅ Status:** Write component tests — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Plan 01-03: Per-Schema themeConfig + LogTape
children.notes:
# Per-Schema themeConfig Overrides + LogTape

Implement per-schema theme overrides and structured logging.

**Requirements:** THEME-05
**Depends on:** 01-01, 01-02

**Done When:**
- Loading schema with `themeConfig` block overrides tokens for that file
- LogTape structured logging foundation in place

**✅ Status:** Per-schema themeConfig overrides apply on file load and the LogTape structured-logging foundation is established.

children.title: Implement per-schema themeConfig parsing
children.notes:
Parse `themeConfig` block from loaded schema JSON. Map `statusColors` and `nodeRadius` to runtime token overrides.

**✅ Status:** Implement per-schema themeConfig parsing — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Override active theme tokens on file load
children.notes:
When a file with `themeConfig` is opened, apply those overrides to the active theme's CSS custom properties. Restore defaults when file is closed.

**✅ Status:** Override active theme tokens on file load — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: LogTape structured logging foundation
children.notes:
Set up LogTape for structured logging across both Bun main process and webview. Configurable log levels, JSON output for production.

**✅ Status:** LogTape structured logging foundation — completed and verified during Phase 01.

children.title: Phase 02 — Read-Only Viewer
children.notes:
# Phase 02: Read-Only Viewer

Any valid JSON schema renders as a fully interactive, read-only tree with correct status badges, file watching, and side panel — with 30 fps performance gate validated.

**Branch:** `gsd/phase-02-read-only-viewer`
**Depends on:** Phase 01
**Plans:** 4/4 complete

## Requirements
- VIEW-01: Zod-validated schema loading
- VIEW-02: Zustand store with `dataKey` pattern
- VIEW-03: Sample schemas
- VIEW-04: react-d3-tree integration with Fit View
- VIEW-05: Custom `RoadmapNode` foreignObject renderer
- VIEW-06: File watcher with debounce
- VIEW-07: `$ref` resolution at load time
- VIEW-08: `.bak.json` backup on file open
- VIEW-09: Data-driven side panel (read-only)
- VIEW-10: Markdown rendering (remark/rehype)
- VIEW-11: 30 fps performance gate (300+ nodes)
- VIEW-12: TB/LR layout toggle
- VIEW-13: Welcome screen
- VIEW-14: Recent files persistence

## Critical Design Decision
`dataKey` only increments on structural mutations (add/delete/move); status-only updates bypass `dataKey` to maintain 30 fps with react-d3-tree.

**✅ Status:** Any valid JSON schema renders as an interactive read-only tree with badges, file watching, and side panel, and the 30 fps performance gate passes.

children.title: Plan 02-01: Schema + Zustand Store Foundation
children.notes:
# Schema + Zustand Store Foundation

Define Zod schemas, create Zustand store with `dataKey` pattern, and create sample schemas.

**Requirements:** VIEW-01, VIEW-02, VIEW-03

**Deliverables:**
- `packages/core/src/schema.ts` — Zod schemas
- `packages/desktop/src/mainview/store/roadmapStore.ts` — Zustand store
- Sample schemas in `samples/`

**Acceptance:**
- Zod schemas validate well-formed JSON, reject malformed with detailed errors
- `roadmapStore` produces react-d3-tree-compatible `treeData`
- `dataKey` stable pattern verified by tests

**✅ Status:** Zod schemas, the dataKey-aware Zustand roadmapStore, and sample schemas are implemented and tested.

children.title: Define Zod schemas in @roadraven/core
children.notes:
Create `RoadmapSchemaSchema`, `RoadmapNodeSchema`, `NodeStatusSchema` with recursive children support via Zod getter pattern.

**✅ Status:** Define Zod schemas in @roadraven/core — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Create Zustand roadmapStore with dataKey pattern
children.notes:
Implement `roadmapStore` with `loadSchema()`, `updateNodeStatus()`, `selectNode()`. Critical: `dataKey` increments on `loadSchema`, NOT on `updateNodeStatus`.

**✅ Status:** Create Zustand roadmapStore with dataKey pattern — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Create sample schemas
children.notes:
Create `hello-world.json` (minimal) and `getting-started.json` (comprehensive) sample schemas in `samples/`.

**✅ Status:** Create sample schemas — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Wire type re-exports through shared/types.ts
children.notes:
Export `NodeStatus`, `RoadmapNode`, `RoadmapSchema`, `StatusConfig` from `shared/types.ts` for cross-process use.

**✅ Status:** Wire type re-exports through shared/types.ts — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Write schema validation tests
children.notes:
Vitest tests: valid JSON passes, malformed JSON rejected with Zod errors, edge cases (empty nodes, deep nesting, missing fields).

**✅ Status:** Write schema validation tests — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Plan 02-02: Tree Renderer + File Watcher + Layout
children.notes:
# Tree Renderer + File Watcher + Layout Toggle

Integrate react-d3-tree, add file watching, `$ref` resolution, backups, and layout toggle.

**Requirements:** VIEW-04, VIEW-05, VIEW-06, VIEW-07, VIEW-08, VIEW-12

**Deliverables:**
- `Canvas.tsx` — react-d3-tree integration
- `RoadmapNode.tsx` — custom foreignObject renderer
- `SchemaErrorPanel.tsx` — Zod validation error display
- `fileWatcher.ts` — Bun-side file watching

**Acceptance:**
- Valid schema renders as interactive tree
- TB/LR toggle works and persists per file
- File watcher detects external changes and re-renders
- Error panel shows Zod validation failures

**✅ Status:** react-d3-tree renderer, file watcher, $ref resolution, .bak backup, TB/LR layout toggle, and the schema error panel all work.

children.title: Integrate react-d3-tree with dataKey and Fit View
children.notes:
Wire `roadmapStore.treeData` into react-d3-tree's `<Tree>` component. Use `dataKey` for controlled re-renders. Implement Fit View reset on schema load.

**✅ Status:** Integrate react-d3-tree with dataKey and Fit View — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Custom RoadmapNode foreignObject renderer
children.notes:
Custom `renderCustomNodeElement` using `foreignObject` for rich HTML node rendering. Shows title, status badge with color, and type indicator.

**✅ Status:** Custom RoadmapNode foreignObject renderer — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: File watcher with debounce (Bun side)
children.notes:
Bun-side file watcher using `Bun.file.watch()`. Debounced to prevent rapid re-reads. Sends updated schema to webview via RPC on change.

**✅ Status:** File watcher with debounce (Bun side) — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: $ref resolution at load time
children.notes:
Resolve `$ref` pointers when loading schema. External file references resolved relative to schema file path.

**✅ Status:** $ref resolution at load time — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: .bak.json backup on every file open
children.notes:
Create `.bak.json` alongside opened schema file. Safety net for undo (undo/redo deferred to v1.1).

**✅ Status:** .bak.json backup on every file open — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: TB/LR layout toggle with persistence
children.notes:
Top-to-bottom / Left-to-right orientation toggle. Persists preference per file across sessions.

**✅ Status:** TB/LR layout toggle with persistence — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Schema validation error panel
children.notes:
Display Zod validation errors inline when schema is malformed. `SchemaErrorPanel.tsx` shows error paths and messages.

**✅ Status:** Schema validation error panel — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Plan 02-03: Side Panel + Welcome Screen + Recent Files
children.notes:
# Side Panel + Welcome Screen + Recent Files

Data-driven side panel, markdown rendering, welcome screen, and recent files.

**Requirements:** VIEW-09, VIEW-10, VIEW-13, VIEW-14

**Deliverables:**
- `SidePanel.tsx` — data-driven, resizable, pinnable
- `MarkdownRenderer.tsx` — remark + rehype pipeline
- `WelcomeScreen.tsx` — hero, actions, recent files, samples
- `ResizeHandle.tsx` — drag handle

**Acceptance:**
- Side panel shows title, status, type, timestamps, markdown notes
- Resizable 320px–50% viewport width
- Pins on screens >1400px wide
- Recent files list persists (last 10) and opens files on click
- Sample links work

**✅ Status:** Data-driven side panel, markdown renderer, welcome screen, resize handle, auto-pin, and recent-files persistence are complete.

children.title: Build data-driven SidePanel
children.notes:
Read-only side panel showing selected node's title, status, type, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, and markdown notes. Resizable with drag handle, 320px min to 50% viewport max.

**✅ Status:** Build data-driven SidePanel — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Implement markdown renderer (remark/rehype)
children.notes:
Markdown rendering pipeline using remark (parse) + rehype (HTML). Supports headings, code blocks, links, lists, inline formatting.

**✅ Status:** Implement markdown renderer (remark/rehype) — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Build WelcomeScreen
children.notes:
Welcome screen with hero section, action buttons (Open File, New Roadmap), recent files list, and sample roadmap links.

**✅ Status:** Build WelcomeScreen — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Build ResizeHandle component
children.notes:
Drag handle for resizing the side panel. Visual feedback on hover/drag. Constrains between min/max widths.

**✅ Status:** Build ResizeHandle component — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Implement auto-pin on wide screens
children.notes:
Side panel auto-pins (stays open) on screens wider than 1400px. Toggleable on narrower screens.

**✅ Status:** Implement auto-pin on wide screens — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Implement recent files persistence
children.notes:
Persist last 10 opened files across sessions. Display in welcome screen and File menu. Click to open.

**✅ Status:** Implement recent files persistence — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Plan 02-04: Performance Gate (30 fps)
children.notes:
# Performance Gate — 30 fps Benchmark

300+ visible nodes with 10 simulated `updateNodeStatus()` calls/sec must maintain ≥30 fps.

**Requirements:** VIEW-11

**Deliverables:**
- `packages/desktop/tests/bench/perf.bench.ts` — Vitest benchmark
- `packages/desktop/tests/bench/generateSchema.ts` — 300+ node generator
- `packages/desktop/tests/unit/ui/viewer-smoke.test.tsx` — smoke test

**Acceptance:**
- 300+ node schema loads without crash
- `dataKey` does NOT change during `updateNodeStatus` calls
- Benchmark green and repeatable in CI
- Manual checkpoint confirms 30 fps (D-09 — human verified ✓)

**✅ Status:** The 30 fps performance gate passed against a 300+ node benchmark with dataKey stability verified.

children.title: Build schema generator (300+ nodes)
children.notes:
`generateLargeSchema()` function that produces a valid roadmap JSON with 300+ nodes for benchmarking. Configurable depth and breadth.

**✅ Status:** Build schema generator (300+ nodes) — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Vitest benchmark: dataKey stability
children.notes:
Benchmark measuring `dataKey` stability under sustained `updateNodeStatus()` calls. Verifies `dataKey` does NOT increment on status-only updates.

**✅ Status:** Vitest benchmark: dataKey stability — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: UI smoke test with node count assertion
children.notes:
Smoke test loading 300+ node schema and asserting all nodes render. Catches rendering crashes and memory issues.

**✅ Status:** UI smoke test with node count assertion — completed and verified during Phase 02.

children.title: Manual Playwright fps checkpoint (D-09)
children.notes:
Human-verified performance checkpoint. Manual Playwright test confirming 30 fps on rendered 300+ node tree.

**Decision D-09:** fps gate approved by human reviewer on 2026-04-15.

**✅ Status:** Manual Playwright fps checkpoint confirmed smooth rendering under live updates.

children.title: Phase 03 — Full Editor
children.notes:
# Phase 03: Full Editor

A complete roadmap can be created, edited, and saved without touching JSON directly — with full keyboard control, autosave, atomic writes, and correct `$ref` write-back.

**Depends on:** Phase 02
**Plans:** 4/4

## Requirements
EDIT-01 through EDIT-18

## Key Design Decisions
- Inline rename, add/delete/duplicate/move via keyboard and context menu
- Non-leaf delete shows confirmation dialog with child count
- Copy/paste preserves full subtree
- CodeMirror 6 for markdown editing
- Atomic writes: `.tmp` → rename on all platforms; Windows retry loop (3 attempts, 50ms)
- Debounced 2s write, 30s periodic autosave, flush on `before-quit`
- Undo/Redo deferred to v1.1

**✅ Status:** A full roadmap can be created, edited, and saved without touching JSON — keyboard control, context menu, CodeMirror editor, autosave, and atomic $ref write-back all working.

children.title: Plan 03-01: Node Mutation Operations
children.notes:
# Node Mutation Operations

All CRUD operations on nodes: rename, add, delete, duplicate, copy/paste, move, status change, tree navigation.

**Key Behaviors:**
- Inline rename with Enter/Escape
- Add child/sibling nodes
- Delete with confirmation for non-leaf nodes (shows child count)
- Duplicate node (deep copy with new UUIDs)
- Copy/paste preserves full subtree
- Move node (drag-and-drop or keyboard)
- Status cycling via keyboard shortcut
- Arrow-key tree navigation

**✅ Status:** Inline rename, add/delete/duplicate/move, copy-paste subtree, status cycling, and arrow-key navigation all work via keyboard and menu.

children.title: Inline rename (Enter/Escape)
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Inline rename (Enter/Escape) — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Add child/sibling node
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Add child/sibling node — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Delete node with confirmation dialog
children.notes:
Non-leaf delete shows confirmation dialog with child count. Leaf nodes delete immediately.

**✅ Status:** Delete node with confirmation dialog — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Duplicate node (deep copy, new UUIDs)
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Duplicate node (deep copy, new UUIDs) — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Copy/paste full subtree
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Copy/paste full subtree — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Move node (drag-and-drop + keyboard)
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Move node (drag-and-drop + keyboard) — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Status cycling via keyboard shortcut
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Status cycling via keyboard shortcut — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Arrow-key tree navigation
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Arrow-key tree navigation — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Plan 03-02: Context Menu + Linux Fallback
children.notes:
# Context Menu + Linux Fallback

Right-click context menu for all node operations. Custom fallback for Linux where `ContextMenu.showContextMenu()` is a no-op.

**Requirements:**
- Appears within 50ms of right-click
- Fully keyboard-navigable
- ARIA-compliant
- Linux fallback using HTML/CSS menu

**✅ Status:** Radix-based ARIA context menu plus a Linux webview-rendered fallback menu are implemented.

children.title: Native context menu implementation
children.notes:
Right-click triggers native context menu via Electrobun `ContextMenu.showContextMenu()`. Must appear within 50ms.

**✅ Status:** Native context menu implementation — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Keyboard-navigable menu (ARIA-compliant)
children.notes:
Full keyboard navigation: arrow keys, Enter to select, Escape to dismiss. ARIA roles and labels for accessibility.

**✅ Status:** Keyboard-navigable menu (ARIA-compliant) — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Linux HTML/CSS fallback menu
children.notes:
Custom HTML/CSS context menu for Linux where native `ContextMenu.showContextMenu()` is a no-op. Matches native styling.

**✅ Status:** Linux HTML/CSS fallback menu — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Plan 03-03: Side Panel Editor (CodeMirror 6)
children.notes:
# Side Panel Editor

Replace read-only side panel with editable view. CodeMirror 6 for markdown notes editing, metadata table for structured fields.

**Key Features:**
- CodeMirror 6 markdown editor
- Autosave debounced 1s
- Metadata table (key-value editor)
- Type and status dropdowns

**✅ Status:** CodeMirror 6 markdown editor (Edit/Preview/Split) with editable metadata, title, status, and type is wired into the side panel.

children.title: Integrate CodeMirror 6 markdown editor
children.notes:
Replace markdown viewer with CodeMirror 6 editor. Support markdown syntax highlighting, keyboard shortcuts, and basic formatting toolbar.

**✅ Status:** Integrate CodeMirror 6 markdown editor — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Implement 1s debounced autosave for notes
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Implement 1s debounced autosave for notes — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Build metadata key-value editor
children.notes:
Editable metadata table. Add/remove key-value pairs. Values support string, number, boolean types.

**✅ Status:** Build metadata key-value editor — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Type and status dropdown selectors
children.notes:
Dropdown selectors populated from schema's `statusConfig` and `typeConfig` arrays.

**✅ Status:** Type and status dropdown selectors — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Plan 03-04: Autosave + Atomic Writes + $ref
children.notes:
# Autosave + Atomic Writes + $ref Write-Back

Persistence layer with atomic writes, autosave, and `$ref` mutation routing.

**Key Design:**
- 2s debounced write after any mutation
- 30s periodic autosave
- Flush on `before-quit`
- `.tmp` → rename atomic write (Windows: 3 attempts, 50ms apart)
- Status bar shows: Saved ✓ / Saving... / Error saving
- `$ref` mutations write to correct originating file
- Cross-boundary moves blocked with error

**✅ Status:** Debounced + periodic autosave, atomic .tmp→rename writes, save indicator, and $ref write-back with cross-boundary blocking are complete.

children.title: Implement 2s debounced autosave
children.notes:
Debounced 2s write after any mutation. Resets timer on each change.

**✅ Status:** Implement 2s debounced autosave — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Implement 30s periodic autosave
children.notes: **✅ Status:** Implement 30s periodic autosave — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Flush on before-quit
children.notes:
Register `before-quit` handler to flush any pending writes before app closes. No unsaved-changes confirmation dialog (by design).

**✅ Status:** Flush on before-quit — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Atomic write (.tmp → rename)
children.notes:
Write to `.tmp` file first, then atomic rename. Windows retry loop: 3 attempts, 50ms apart, to handle file locking.

**✅ Status:** Atomic write (.tmp → rename) — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Status bar save indicator
children.notes:
Status bar shows: `Saved ✓` / `Saving...` / `Error saving` with appropriate visual feedback.

**✅ Status:** Status bar save indicator — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: $ref write-back to originating files
children.notes:
When editing nodes loaded via `$ref`, mutations write back to the correct originating file, not the parent schema.

**✅ Status:** $ref write-back to originating files — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Block cross-boundary $ref moves
children.notes:
Moving a node across `$ref` boundaries is blocked with an error message. Nodes stay within their originating file.

**✅ Status:** Block cross-boundary $ref moves — completed and verified during Phase 03.

children.title: Phase 04 — Event API
children.notes:
# Phase 04: Event API

App receives live node status updates from external producers via WebSocket and routes them within 100ms — with Claude Code MCP wrapper as reference producer.

**Depends on:** Phase 03
**Plans:** 4/4

## Requirements
PLUG-01 through PLUG-09

## Event Contract
```json
{
  "nodeId": "uuid",
  "status": "in-progress",
  "meta": { "key": "value" },
  "source": "claude-code"
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- WebSocket server on `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`
- 100ms debounce buffer on Bun side
- `plugin` and `subscribe` schema fields parsed but not acted on (deferred to v1.1)
- Animated pulse on nodes receiving events
- Non-blocking toasts for errors
- In-app event log accessible from View menu

**✅ Status:** Nodes receive live status updates from external producers over WebSocket within 100ms, with the Claude Code MCP wrapper working end-to-end as the reference producer.

children.title: Plan 04-01: Research Gate (Design Document)
children.notes:
# Research Gate

Design document covering all Event API internals. **No implementation until approved.**

**Topics:**
- WebSocket lifecycle (connect, heartbeat, reconnect, close)
- Event contract specification
- 100ms debounce buffer design
- Connection-drop handling
- Malformed-event handling
- Event log storage format

**✅ Status:** Research gate produced the Event API design covering WS lifecycle, event contract, debounce, errors, and log storage.

children.title: WebSocket lifecycle design
children.notes:
Define connect, heartbeat, reconnect, and graceful close behavior.

**✅ Status:** WebSocket lifecycle design — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Event contract specification
children.notes:
Finalize `{ nodeId, status, meta?, source?, timestamp? }` contract with validation rules.

**✅ Status:** Event contract specification — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: 100ms debounce buffer design
children.notes:
Design buffer that batches rapid events per-node within 100ms window. Last-write-wins within window.

**✅ Status:** 100ms debounce buffer design — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Error handling strategy
children.notes:
Define behavior for: connection drops, malformed events, unknown nodeIds, server restart.

**✅ Status:** Error handling strategy — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Event log storage format
children.notes:
Define in-memory event log structure. Rotation policy, max entries, export format.

**✅ Status:** Event log storage format — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Plan 04-02: WebSocket Server + Event Routing
children.notes:
# WebSocket Server + Event Routing

Bun-side WebSocket server receiving events and routing to node store.

**Key Details:**
- `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` — port visible to user
- Event contract validation (Zod)
- 100ms debounce buffer on Bun side
- `plugin` and `subscribe` fields parsed but not acted on
- Route validated events to `roadmapStore.updateNodeStatus()`

**✅ Status:** Bun WebSocket server validates events with Zod, coalesces on a 100ms trailing edge, and routes to the store with a .events.jsonl sidecar.

children.title: Bun WebSocket server setup
children.notes:
Start WebSocket server alongside app on `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`. Display port in status bar.

**✅ Status:** Bun WebSocket server setup — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Event contract validation (Zod)
children.notes:
Validate incoming events against Zod schema. Reject malformed events with descriptive errors.

**✅ Status:** Event contract validation (Zod) — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: 100ms debounce buffer implementation
children.notes:
Buffer rapid events per-node within 100ms window. Flush last value to store after window expires.

**✅ Status:** 100ms debounce buffer implementation — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Parse plugin and subscribe schema fields
children.notes:
Parse `plugin` and `subscribe` blocks from schema without acting on them. Validate structure but defer behavior to v1.1.

**✅ Status:** Parse plugin and subscribe schema fields — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Route events to roadmapStore
children.notes:
Valid events routed to `roadmapStore.updateNodeStatus()` via RPC. Unknown `nodeId` events produce non-blocking toast.

**✅ Status:** Route events to roadmapStore — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Plan 04-03: Event API UI
children.notes:
# Event API UI

Visual feedback for live events: animated pulse, integration zone in side panel, toasts, event log.

**Deliverables:**
- Animated pulse on nodes receiving events
- Side panel Integration zone (connection status, last event, last meta)
- Non-blocking error toasts
- Event log in View menu

**✅ Status:** Pulse indicator, side-panel Integration zone, non-blocking error toasts, and the event-log viewer are all implemented.

children.title: Animated pulse on event-receiving nodes
children.notes:
CSS animation pulse on nodes that have an active event producer. Visual indicator of live connection.

**✅ Status:** Animated pulse on event-receiving nodes — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Side panel Integration zone
children.notes:
New section in side panel showing: connection status (connected/disconnected), last event timestamp, last meta as key-value table.

**✅ Status:** Side panel Integration zone — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Non-blocking error toasts
children.notes:
Toast notifications for: malformed events, unknown nodeIds, connection drops. Auto-dismiss after 5s. Non-blocking.

**✅ Status:** Non-blocking error toasts — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Event log viewer (View menu)
children.notes:
In-app event log showing all received events with timestamp, nodeId, status, source. Accessible from View menu.

**✅ Status:** Event log viewer (View menu) — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Plan 04-04: Claude Code MCP Wrapper
children.notes:
# Claude Code MCP Wrapper

Reference Event Producer in `plugins/claude-code/`. Wraps event contract as MCP tools for Claude Code integration.

**Deliverables:**
- MCP tool definitions for status updates
- WebSocket client connecting to RoadRaven
- End-to-end test: Claude Code → MCP → WebSocket → node badge update

**✅ Status:** The Claude Code MCP wrapper connects and updates node status end-to-end as the reference producer.

children.title: MCP tool definitions for event contract
children.notes:
Define MCP tools: `update_node_status`, `get_roadmap_nodes`, `subscribe_to_node`. Maps to WebSocket event contract.

**✅ Status:** MCP tool definitions for event contract — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: WebSocket client for MCP wrapper
children.notes:
WebSocket client in `plugins/claude-code/` connecting to `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`. Handles reconnection and connection status.

**✅ Status:** WebSocket client for MCP wrapper — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: End-to-end integration test
children.notes:
Full pipeline test: Claude Code invokes MCP tool → MCP sends WebSocket event → RoadRaven updates node badge. Verify within 100ms.

**✅ Status:** End-to-end integration test — completed and verified during Phase 04.

children.title: Phase 05 — Packaging & Distribution
children.notes:
# Phase 05: Packaging & Distribution

Native installers build for Windows (.zip with -Setup.exe) and Linux (.tar.gz), npm packages publish at lockstep version, the GitHub Pages docs site is live, and the accessibility audit passes.

**Depends on:** Phase 04
**Plans:** 2/2

## Requirements
- PACK-01 through PACK-06: Packaging and distribution

## Key Deliverables
- Native installers: Windows `.zip` (-Setup.exe) + Linux `.tar.gz` self-extracting bundle
- Auto-updater: stable channel (canary deferred to v1.1)
- npm packages: `@roadraven/core` + `@roadraven/plugin-claude-code`
- Accessibility audit (axe-core + manual checklist)
- Documentation site + CONTRIBUTING + plugin authoring guide

## Dropped from scope
- HTML / PNG export (EXPO-*) — removed; not part of v1.0
- macOS `.dmg` and Ubuntu `.deb` — not shipped
- `@roadraven/react` — deferred to v1.1

**✅ Status:** Native Windows .zip and Linux .tar.gz installers build via tag-triggered CI, @roadraven/core and the Claude Code plugin publish to npm, the docs site is live, and the accessibility audit passed (PNG/HTML export was dropped from scope).

metadata.requirements: EXPO-01 through EXPO-03, PACK-01 through PACK-06

children.title: Plan 05-03: Packaging + Auto-Updater
children.notes:
# Packaging + Auto-Updater

Native installers for all three platforms and Electrobun auto-updater.

**Installers:**
- macOS: `.dmg`
- Windows: `.exe`
- Ubuntu: `.deb`

**Auto-Updater:**
- Canary + stable channels
- `bundleCEF: true` on all platforms (confirmed from Phase 00)

**Other:**
- All actions reachable via keyboard + toolbar
- SIGTERM handler for graceful shutdown

**✅ Status:** Tag-triggered release workflow builds Windows .zip and Linux .tar.gz with the Electrobun auto-updater configured (macOS .dmg and .deb were dropped from scope).

children.notes: **⏸ Status:** Deferred — macOS packaging was never shipped (out of v1.0 scope).

children.notes: **✅ Status:** Shipped as a Windows .zip containing -Setup.exe via tag-triggered CI.

children.notes:
Confirm `bundleCEF: true` works on Linux builds.

**✅ Status:** Linux packaging shipped as a self-extracting .tar.gz bundle (.deb format not used).

children.notes:
Configure Electrobun auto-updater with canary and stable channel resolution.

**✅ Status:** Stable-channel auto-updater configured; canary channel deferred to v1.1.

children.notes: **✅ Status:** SIGTERM graceful shutdown handler — completed and verified during Phase 05.

children.notes:
# npm Packages + Accessibility + Docs

Publish npm packages, accessibility audit, and documentation.

**npm Packages:**
- `@roadmap-viewer/core` — zero desktop dependencies (CI enforces)
- `@roadmap-viewer/react` — peerDependencies on React

**Accessibility:**
- Full accessibility audit
- Keyboard-navigable tree and menus
- Screen reader support

**Documentation:**
- README with getting started guide
- Plugin development guide
- API reference

**✅ Status:** @roadraven/core and the Claude Code plugin publish to npm, the GitHub Pages docs site is live, and the accessibility audit passed (@roadraven/react deferred to v1.1).

children.title: Publish @roadmap-viewer/core
children.notes:
Publish `@roadmap-viewer/core` to npm. Zero desktop dependencies enforced by CI check. Contains schema types and validation.

**✅ Status:** Published to npm as @roadraven/core (renamed from @roadmap-viewer/core).

children.title: Publish @roadmap-viewer/react
children.notes:
Publish `@roadmap-viewer/react` with React as peerDependency. Contains tree renderer and UI components.

**⏸ Status:** Deferred to v1.1 — the React bindings package was not published in v1.0.

children.title: Accessibility audit
children.notes:
Full accessibility audit: keyboard navigation, screen reader labels, focus management, color contrast (all themes), ARIA roles.

**✅ Status:** Accessibility audit — completed and verified during Phase 05.

children.title: Write README and documentation
children.notes:
Getting started guide, feature overview, keyboard shortcuts reference.

**✅ Status:** Write README and documentation — completed and verified during Phase 05.

children.title: Write plugin development guide
children.notes:
Guide for creating Event API producers. WebSocket contract, MCP wrapper pattern, example plugin.

**✅ Status:** Write plugin development guide — completed and verified during Phase 05.

children.title: Phase 06 — Agentic Roadmap Authoring
children.notes:
# Phase 06: Agentic Roadmap Authoring

Agents (Claude Code and other MCP-capable LLMs) can read, create, edit, move, and delete roadmap nodes via the MCP wrapper — turning Phase 4's one-way producer→app pipe into a bidirectional contract.

**Depends on:** Phase 04
**Plans:** 6/6

## Outcome
19 MCP tools, a 4-gate safety model (kill-switch, path allowlist, cross-ref boundary, cascade/cycle), a 13-code error taxonomy, and a drawer audit trail — all contract-tested (TDD).

**✅ Status:** Agents can read, create, edit, move, and delete roadmap nodes via 19 MCP tools behind a 4-gate safety model — shipped 2026-05-10 with all review findings fixed and UAT signed off.
